name: Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana
name_original: Cueva de San Genado – trail running desde Peñalba de Santiago
description: Run 2.8 miles (4.4 km) to the historic San Genadio Cave from Peñalba de Santiago, gaining 515 feet (157 metres) on this difficult trail.
region_intro: Jogging around Ponferrada offers a diverse landscape, from gentle riverside paths along the Sil River to challenging mountain trails in the Montes de León and Sierra de los Ancares. The region is characterized by its unique Roman mining landscapes, such as Las Médulas, and verdant valleys, providing varied terrain for runners. These natural features, combined with historical sites, create a compelling environment for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Ponferrada The most popular jogging route is Río Sil Trail loop from Ponferrada, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 53 minutes to complete. This route offers mostly paved surfaces along the river, suitable for moderate runs. Another top favourite among local runners is La Mirada Circular Natural Trail, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This trail features waterfalls and historical sites, providing a scenic running experience. Local runners also love the Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip from Puerto de los Portillinos through Montes Aquilianos, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through the Montes Aquilianos, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Jogging around Ponferrada is defined by Roman mining landscapes, verdant river valleys, and challenging mountain trails. The extensive network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy jogs to difficult trail runs. The routes in Ponferrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Ponferrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes running in Ponferrada a unique experience?
A: Running in Ponferrada offers a diverse landscape, from gentle riverside paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Sil River&lt;/strong&gt; to challenging mountain trails in the &lt;strong&gt;Montes de León&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de los Ancares&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is particularly known for its unique Roman mining landscapes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/a&gt;, and verdant valleys, providing varied terrain for all runners. These natural features, combined with historical sites, create a compelling environment for outdoor activity.

Q: How many running routes are available around Ponferrada?
A: There are over 70 distinct running routes around Ponferrada documented on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy jogs to more difficult trail runs, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, Ponferrada offers several easier running paths. For a moderate and mostly paved surface, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Sil Trail loop from Ponferrada&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, covering about 8.2 km along the river.

Q: What kind of challenging trail running options are available for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Ponferrada has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip through Montes Aquilianos&lt;/a&gt; is an 12.8 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pajariel Peak – Boeza Bridge loop from Ponferrada&lt;/a&gt;, which is a hard 15.5 km route.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature waterfalls or other natural attractions?
A: Absolutely! The region around Ponferrada is home to beautiful natural features. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.1 km path that features waterfalls and historical sites. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1617842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervencia Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Ponferrada?
A: Yes, many routes in Ponferrada are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.1 km loop. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 4.4 km trail.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ponferrada?
A: The running routes in Ponferrada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: Yes, Ponferrada's rich history is reflected in its trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; includes historical sites. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt; leads to the historic San Genadio Cave from Peñalba de Santiago. The city also serves as a key stop on the &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago (French Way)&lt;/strong&gt;, offering paths that combine natural beauty with historical significance.

Q: Can I incorporate parts of the Camino de Santiago into my run around Ponferrada?
A: Yes, Ponferrada is a significant stop on the &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago (French Way)&lt;/strong&gt;. While leaving the city can involve some urban areas, just a few kilometers outside, the routes quickly transition into scenic vineyards and countryside, providing excellent opportunities to run along sections of this historic pilgrimage path.

Q: Are there running routes that offer views of the Montes de León?
A: Definitely. The mountainous terrain surrounding Ponferrada, including the &lt;strong&gt;Montes de León&lt;/strong&gt;, offers breathtaking vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip through Montes Aquilianos&lt;/a&gt; provide challenging trails and expansive views of these mountains. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montes de León&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Ponferrada?
A: The terrain around Ponferrada is highly varied. You can find mostly paved surfaces along the &lt;strong&gt;Sil River&lt;/strong&gt;, as well as rugged trails with significant elevation gain in the mountainous areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Montes de León&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de los Ancares&lt;/strong&gt;. The unique 'Martian landscape' of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/a&gt; also offers distinct red cliffs and chestnut-filled trails.
tips: The Arroyo del Silencio Waterfall and Bridge are located near Peñalba de Santiago, in the region of El Bierzo. This route is known for its natural beauty and tranquility, offering a unique experience for hiking enthusiasts.

We are in the cave of San Genado located in a beautiful enclave. The views towards the town of Santiago de Peñalba are fantastic, as is the route that leaves from it. The cave is conditioned as a hermitage and it is not very big. The various paths that exist are well indicated.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cueva de San Genadio trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the picturesque village of Peñalba de Santiago. You can usually find parking within or just outside the village, which serves as the main access point for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva de San Genadio trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: While the trail is often described as having a low to medium-low difficulty for hiking, for trail running, it's considered more challenging due to its varied terrain and some steep uphill sections. It's best suited for runners accustomed to varied terrain and inclines, making it a more difficult run than a casual jog.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows a dirt path, but you'll encounter stony sections, especially as you approach the cave. There are also wooden bridges and narrow footpaths. A notable feature is a river crossing near the cave, which can be quite stony and challenging if water levels are high.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll run through the beautiful Valle del Silencio, alongside a river, and through a lovely shaded forest. Key points of interest include the Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio and the historic San Genadio Cave itself. From the cave, you'll get breathtaking panoramic views of the valley and Peñalba de Santiago.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to trail run this route?
faqs.answer: This trail is approximately 4.4 kilometers (2.75 miles) long. For trail running, it typically takes around 38 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cueva de San Genadio trail is often completed as a circular route, starting and ending in Peñalba de Santiago, allowing you to experience the full beauty of the Valle del Silencio.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva de San Genadio trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain, potential for wildlife, and to respect other trail users and the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Cueva de San Genadio trail or the cave itself. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio is particularly impressive with higher water levels. The trail's shaded sections make it comfortable even on sunny days, but always check local weather conditions, especially regarding potential heavy rains that can affect the river crossing.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with sections of other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the La Tebaida Berciana (85% of the route), Camino Natural La Mirada Circular (85%), and Canales Romanos de las Médulas (72%).

faqs.question: What should I know about the river crossing near the cave?
faqs.answer: There is a river crossing close to the San Genadio Cave. This section can be quite challenging with many stones, especially if water levels are high after heavy rains. It's advisable to exercise caution and wear appropriate waterproof footwear, or consider avoiding the trail immediately after significant rainfall.

waypoints.name: Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio
